process.title: Adding Parts To Inventory
process.description: Steps taken to add parts to inventory.
process.usedWhen: Replacement parts are received for cases repaired using inventory parts, inventory is being replenished, or parts are purchased specifically for inventory.

nodes.label: Open and log in to the Inventory System
Navigate to the Check In tab
Enter received part details
details: Case Number: The case number the part was received for
Order Number: The order number associated with the received part
tasks: Case Number: The case number the part was received for
Order Number: The order number associated with the received part

nodes.label: Inventory System adds the part to inventory
If presented with a validation message, correct part number or escalate
details: If the entry is incorrect, click Cancel, correct the part number, and click Check Part In again

details.text: If entry is correct and the part must be added to inventory
subItems: In the prompt, enter the reason the part needs to be added to inventory and click Send Request
Management reviews request and approves or denies it

tasks: If the entry is incorrect, click Cancel, correct the part number, and click Check Part In again
If entry is correct and the part must be added to inventory, click Escalate to Supervisor
In the prompt, enter the reason the part needs to be added to inventory and click Send Request
Management reviews request and approves or denies it

nodes.label: Repeat the process for all parts requiring check-in
Print the Check-in Session report to support shelving

details.text: Admin Tip: This report can be used to streamline shelving by allowing parts to be checked in first and stocked afterward, rather than shelving each part individually.

tasks: Use Print This Check-in Session to generate the report
Report allows parts to be checked in first and stocked afterward

nodes.label: This report can be used to streamline shelving by allowing parts to be checked in first and stocked afterward, rather than shelving each part individually.
